channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Getting the Israel-Hamas ceasefire to the next phase will be difficult. Here's why
item.description: Israel and Hamas have begun working to advance their ceasefire agreement in Gaza to the next phase, but it's unclear if they'll get there and, if not, what comes next.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bangladeshi students who led uprising that ousted ex-premier Sheikh Hasina form new political party
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Students in Bangladesh who led a mass uprising to topple former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina last summer are now diving into politics by forming the new National Citizen Party. The aim is to create new political space in a fiercely divisive dynastic political landscape.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump greets Zelensky at White House for high-stakes meeting
item.description: President Trump welcomed Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to the White House on Friday for a highly anticipated meeting amid Trump’s push to end the war in Ukraine. Trump shook hands with the Ukrainian president outside the West Wing, quipping that Zelensky was “all dressed up.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: A Myanmar online news site says its jailed journalist is tortured for revealing abuses of prisoners
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An independent online news agency in Myanmar said Friday that one of its journalists arrested two years ago has been subjected to daily physical and mental abuse after exposing human rights violations in the country’s main prison where he's being held.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freight train derails in Czech Republic, causing major chemical fire
item.description: Czech firefighters are battling a major chemical blaze in eastern Czech Republic after a freight train derailed and carcinogenic benzol from damaged tank wagons caught fire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China accuses US of 'blackmail' after Trump warns of increased tariffs
item.description: China’s officials condemned President Trump for his decision to add a 10 percent tariff on Chinese imports over the country’s alleged complicity in the international smuggling of fentanyl, a deadly synthetic drug. “The fentanyl issue is just an excuse the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy in Thailand warns Americans of 'violent retaliatory attacks' risk after Uyghurs deported to China
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Bangkok is warning Americans of potential "violent retaliatory attacks" after a group of Uyghurs were deported by Thailand to China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN talks end in Rome with nations backing $200 billion a year plan to protect nature
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Global negotiators concluded an extended session of the United Nations biodiversity conference, COP16, with key commitments on funds needed and the institutions through which the funds will be channeled to protect the world's biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mourners bury one of the last hostages released from Gaza as talks start for ceasefire future
item.description: Mourners are burying the remains of one of the last hostages released in the first phase of the ceasefire between Hamas militants and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A good rapport but mixed signals on Ukraine: Takeaways from Keir Starmer's trip to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prime Minister Keir Starmer wore a broad smile aboard the plane home from Washington. He landed back in Britain on Friday with the satisfaction of a tricky mission not quite accomplished, but off to a flying start.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — When Israeli snipers took positions in her neighborhood, Haleemeh Zawaydeh knew her family needed to leave quickly. As the snipers' gunfire rang out, the 63-year-old matriarch said there was no time to pack as she and 14 other family members fled on foot.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, AREF TUFAHA and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge overthrows conviction of owners of New Zealand island where 22 died in volcanic eruption
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — The owners of an island volcano in New Zealand where 22 tourists and local guides died in an eruption had their criminal conviction for failing to keep visitors safe thrown out by a judge on Friday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico extradites dozens of cartel leaders and members to US, including drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
item.description: As Mexico and the U.S. negotiate trade tariffs, the Mexican government has handed over 29 cartel leaders and members who were wanted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea sending more troops to Russia, South Korea says
item.description: North Korea appears to have sent more troops to Russia despite its soldiers suffering heavy casualties fighting on the front lines in Ukraine, South Korea’s main spy agency said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Baier to interview Zelensky after Trump meeting
item.description: Bret Baier has secured the first one-on-one interview with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky after his meeting with President Trump at the White House, to discuss the ongoing Ukraine-Russia conflict.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Republicans block measure condemning Trump administration vote on UN Russia resolution
item.description: Senate Republicans on Thursday objected to an effort by Democrats to condemn the Trump administration's decision to vote against a United Nations resolution laying blame for the war in Ukraine at the feet of Russia. Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: King Charles invites Trump to UK for a state visit
item.description: King Charles III has invited President Trump for a state visit to the United Kingdom, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the president during their meeting Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russian and US diplomats meet in Istanbul for second round of talks
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Russian and U.S. diplomats met in Istanbul on Thursday to discuss normalizing the operation of their respective embassies after years expelling each others' diplomats.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schiff: Millions of Americans 'downright ashamed' of Trump
item.description: Sen. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.), a longtime critic of President Trump, said he thinks millions of Americans are ashamed of the president “for the first time” over his efforts to strike a deal with Ukraine to gain access to its mineral wealth.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: House lawmakers want Trump to pressure EU to sanction Iran military group
item.description: Two House lawmakers are calling on President Trump to pressure the European Union to designate as a terrorist organization Iran’s premier military force, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). Reps. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) and Brad Schneider (D-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis' slight kidney insufficiency has receded as he battles pneumonia, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis slept well and was resting Thursday morning as his doctors say the 88-year-old pontiff has shown further slight improvement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU beat COVID and ditched Russian energy. Now it must learn to live without US security help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Over the last five years, European Union countries have been forced to adapt to unprecedented circumstances. They pulled together to purchase tens of millions of vaccines and devised an innovative debt financing scheme to resuscitate their COVID-19-ravaged economies.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 84 Indonesians taken out of scam centers in Myanmar are set to go home
item.description: MAE SOT, Thailand (AP) — Eighty-four Indonesians freed from scam centers in Myanmar arrived at the Thai border city of Mae Sot earlier on Thursday in an ongoing repatriation effort that follows a crackdown by Thailand, Myanmar and China.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Andrew Tate, who faces rape, trafficking charges in Romania, has left for US
item.description: The brothers are also charged with forming a criminal gang to sexually exploit women. Andrew Tate also faces an additional charge of rape.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran can manufacture 6 nuclear weapons with newly enriched uranium, UN Atomic agency report says
item.description: A report by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) cautioned that the Islamic Republic of Iran is producing a large amount of uranium that could be used in six atomic bombs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews: Investigators
item.description: The suspect arrived in Germany in 2023 as an unaccompanied minor and successfully applied for asylum, investigators said. He lives in Leipzig.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9
